purpose: Provenance-verification ledger for research references cited in Governor experiments. Tier P0=asserted (model memory, unverified), P1=search-verified (citation confirmed via real external retrieval), P2=full-text-verified (primary source retrieved).

capability_boundary.WebSearch: AVAILABLE — real queries return verifiable sources (tested 2026-07-22)
capability_boundary.WebFetch: BLOCKED — HTTP 403 from org egress policy for arxiv.org and sites.math.washington.edu (tested 2026-07-22); full-text P2 verification not reachable this session
capability_boundary.note: Do not claim continuous/autonomous scanning: no scheduler or persistent process exists. Retrieval is on-demand within a session only.

references.citation: Markowitz, H. (1952). Portfolio Selection. Journal of Finance, 7, 77-91.
references.query: Markowitz 1952 Portfolio Selection Journal of Finance mean-variance efficient frontier
references.verified_facts: Venue Journal of Finance, vol 7, pages 77-91, year 1952 confirmed; mean-variance efficient frontier via quadratic programming confirmed. Raised P0->P1 in cycle GOV-EVO-2026-07-D03-001.

references.citation: Rockafellar, R.T. & Uryasev, S. (2000). Optimization of Conditional Value-at-Risk. Journal of Risk, 2, 21-41.
references.query: Rockafellar Uryasev 2000 Optimization of Conditional Value-at-Risk CVaR paper
references.retrieved_source: https://www.scirp.org/reference/referencespapers?referenceid=2282764 ; primary author copy referenced at sites.math.washington.edu/~rtr/papers (full-text fetch 403-blocked)
references.verified_facts: Venue Journal of Risk, vol 2, pages 21-41, year 2000 confirmed by multiple independent search results; CVaR minimization via auxiliary function confirmed.

references.citation: Dowd, K. (2005). Measuring Market Risk (2nd ed.). Wiley. ISBN 978-0-470-01303-8. [CORRECTED from 2007 -> 2005]
references.query: Dowd 2007 Measuring Market Risk second edition Wiley book value at risk
references.verified_facts: CORRECTION: 2nd edition published 2005 (Wiley), NOT 2007 as originally cited in EXP-001. ISBN 978-0-470-01303-8. Provenance verification caught a citation-year error. Raised P0->P1 with correction in cycle GOV-EVO-2026-07-D04-001.

references.citation: BCBS (2016). Minimum Capital Requirements for Market Risk (FRTB) — Expected Shortfall replaces VaR. Basel III.
references.query: Basel III FRTB Fundamental Review of Trading Book expected shortfall market risk capital framework BCBS
references.verified_facts: FRTB finalised Jan 2016 by BCBS; Expected Shortfall (= CVaR) replaces VaR as the mainstream market-risk metric; desk-level model approval. Confirms EXP-001 regulatory-standard reference. Raised P0->P1 in cycle GOV-EVO-2026-07-D04-001.

references.citation: Almgren, R. & Chriss, N. (2000). Optimal Execution of Portfolio Transactions. Journal of Risk, 3(2), 5-39.
references.query: Almgren Chriss 2000 Optimal execution of portfolio transactions market impact model
references.retrieved_source: https://www.smallake.kr/wp-content/uploads/2016/03/optliq.pdf (circulated author copy); multiple independent search results
references.verified_facts: Confirmed: efficient frontier of liquidation strategies, permanent+temporary linear market impact, hyperbolic optimal trajectory, Liquidity-adjusted VaR (L-VaR); year 2000.
